ActivityResponse.description:
Shape of one activity-feed entry — mirrors `ActivityLog.to_dict()`
(models/activity_log.py). OpenAPI/codegen typing only (attached via
`responses=`); the handler returns the plain dict unchanged. `details` is
an opaque JSON column, so it is typed loosely as a dict.
ActivityResponse.title: ActivityResponse

BurndownPoint.description: One day of the analytics burndown series.
BurndownPoint.title: BurndownPoint

CostAnalysisResponse.description:
AI-produced cost breakdown. Best-effort shape — the AI output isn't
validated, but these routes use `responses=` (not `response_model=`), so
this only types the generated client; it never validates/filters at runtime.
Shared by ProjectArchitectureResponse and PRDAnalysisResponse.
CostAnalysisResponse.title: CostAnalysisResponse

GoalResponse.description:
Shape of one project goal — mirrors `ProjectGoal.to_dict()`
(models/project_goal.py). OpenAPI/codegen typing only (attached via
`responses=`); the handler returns the plain dict unchanged.
GoalResponse.title: GoalResponse

GoogleLoginRequest.description: Request model for Google SSO login
GoogleLoginRequest.title: GoogleLoginRequest

MilestoneResponse.description:
Shape of one project milestone — mirrors `ProjectMilestone.to_dict()`
(models/project_milestone.py). OpenAPI/codegen typing only (attached via
`responses=`); the handler returns the plain dict unchanged.
MilestoneResponse.title: MilestoneResponse

MyTaskResponse.description:
Response shape for ``GET /api/workitems/my-tasks``.

OpenAPI/codegen documentation only — the handler returns plain dicts at
runtime. Overlaps heavily with ``WorkItemListResponse`` but is a distinct
shape: it adds ``is_overdue``/``project_id``/``project_name``/
``reporter_name``/``completed_at``, drops ``start_date``/``created_at``/
``updated_at``/``epic`` (string), and — unlike the list endpoint — passes
``estimated_hours``/``logged_hours``/``remaining_hours`` straight from the
(nullable) columns without an ``or 0`` guard, so those are ``int | None``.
``assigned_hours`` and ``story_points`` keep the ``or 0`` default.
MyTaskResponse.title: MyTaskResponse

PRDAnalysisResponse.description:
Shape of `PRDAnalysis.to_dict()` (models/architecture.py). Field
optionality is inferred from `to_dict()` and the underlying nullable
columns: `summary` is a nullable Text column; the JSON list columns are
coalesced to `[]`/`{}`. The AI-produced blobs (cost_analysis / risks /
timeline / recommended_tools) are best-effort shapes — typed for the
generated client only (these routes use `responses=`, never
`response_model=`, so nothing is validated/filtered at runtime).
PRDAnalysisResponse.title: PRDAnalysisResponse

PersonalTaskResponse.description:
OpenAPI/codegen response shape for a single personal task.

Mirrors PersonalTask.to_dict() exactly. Documentation only — the routes
keep returning their existing dicts unchanged at runtime (this is wired via
`responses=`, not `response_model=`, so there is no runtime re-serialization).
Nullability is taken from the to_dict serialization, not the DB columns:
`created_at`/`updated_at` are guarded with `... if ... else None`.
PersonalTaskResponse.title: PersonalTaskResponse

ProjectAnalyticsResponse.description:
OpenAPI response shape for the project analytics endpoint.

Attached via ``responses=`` only — the handler still returns a plain dict, so
there is NO runtime re-serialization. Distribution fields are ``dict[str, int]``
because the builders always emit integer counts.
ProjectAnalyticsResponse.title: ProjectAnalyticsResponse

ProjectArchitectureResponse.description:
Shape of `selected_architecture` — the output of
`Architecture.to_dict()` (models/architecture.py). It is `null` in the
golden (no selected architecture), so field optionality is inferred from
`to_dict()` and the underlying nullable columns rather than the golden.
ProjectArchitectureResponse.title: ProjectArchitectureResponse

ProjectCategoryAssignment.description:
Body for the category-assignment endpoint. `null` clears the
category — making the project uncategorized.
ProjectCategoryAssignment.title: ProjectCategoryAssignment

ProjectDetailResponse.description:
Full project payload returned by the list (`GET /`) and detail
(`GET /{project_id}`) endpoints. Mirrors `format_projects_batch` exactly.
ProjectDetailResponse.title: ProjectDetailResponse

ProjectDeveloperEntry.description: One entry in the `developers` list, built by `_developers_by_project`.
ProjectDeveloperEntry.title: ProjectDeveloperEntry

ProjectWeeklyReportRow.description:
One row of the admin Projects → Weekly Report table.

Counts are work-item totals (not hours). "Done this week" uses
``WorkItem.completed_at`` against the week window returned by
``capacity_service.week_boundaries()`` so it matches the rest of the app's
weekly accounting (Sat 00:00 → Fri 23:59 UTC).
ProjectWeeklyReportRow.title: ProjectWeeklyReportRow

ProjectWorkItemStatsResponse.description:
Shape of the `work_item_stats` block, built by
`get_work_item_stats_batch` / `_empty_stats`.
ProjectWorkItemStatsResponse.title: ProjectWorkItemStatsResponse

PulseOverridePayload.description: Request body for ``PUT /pulse-overrides``. Treated as opaque.
PulseOverridePayload.title: PulseOverridePayload

RoleResponse.description:
Role shape produced by _role_to_dict().

Used for OpenAPI/TS typing only (no runtime serialization). user_count is
omitted by _role_to_dict when None, so it is optional/nullable here.
RoleResponse.title: RoleResponse

SlimWorkItem.description:
Slim payload used by the Kanban board endpoint.

Excludes heavy fields (description, acceptance_criteria, started_at)
that the board view never renders. Wire size is roughly 40-50% of
the full row, which adds up fast on projects with hundreds of items.
SlimWorkItem.title: SlimWorkItem

SprintResponse.description:
OpenAPI response shape for the project sprints list (counts/points enriched).

Attached via ``responses=`` only — the handler still returns plain dicts, so
there is NO runtime re-serialization. Nullability mirrors the Sprint ORM model
and the dict builder in ``list_project_sprints``.
SprintResponse.title: SprintResponse

SprintVelocityPoint.description: One sprint's committed/completed points for the analytics velocity chart.
SprintVelocityPoint.title: SprintVelocityPoint

TeamPerformanceEntry.description: Per-assignee rollup for the analytics team-performance section.
TeamPerformanceEntry.title: TeamPerformanceEntry

TimeBlockResponse.description:
One positioned calendar block, denormalized with its ticket's display
fields so the calendar can render without a second round-trip.
TimeBlockResponse.title: TimeBlockResponse

TimeEntriesResponse.description:
Wraps the rows with a totals strip and a truncation flag so the
frontend can warn when its filters return more than the cap.
TimeEntriesResponse.title: TimeEntriesResponse

TimeEntryRow.description:
One row in the admin time-entries grid. Flattens the WorkItem and
Developer joins so the frontend can render without nested lookups.
TimeEntryRow.title: TimeEntryRow

UpdateTimeBlockRequest.description:
Move / resize / reassign. All fields optional — send only what changed.

``start_time``/``end_time`` move or resize the block (hours re-derived);
``work_item_id`` reassigns the block to a different ticket (rolls up hours
on both the old and new ticket).
UpdateTimeBlockRequest.title: UpdateTimeBlockRequest

UserListItemResponse.description:
Rich admin user shape returned by GET /api/auth/admin/users.

Mirrors User.to_dict() plus the github_username joined from the linked
Developer row. Used for OpenAPI/TS typing only (no runtime serialization).
UserListItemResponse.title: UserListItemResponse

WeeklyTicket.description:
Compact ticket shape for the Projects → Reports drill-down.

Designed to keep the response small: only the fields the table needs to
render. Full ticket detail (description, comments, etc.) lives behind the
project board.
WeeklyTicket.title: WeeklyTicket

WorkItemDetailResponse.description:
Response shape for ``GET /api/workitems/{id}`` (the side-panel detail).

Documents the wire format for OpenAPI/codegen only — the handler returns a
plain column dict at runtime, so this is never used to re-serialize. Unlike
the list endpoints, this one returns the *raw* model columns with no
normalization: ``id`` stays an int (not stringified), dates are serialized
by FastAPI's jsonable_encoder, and there is no ``or 0`` / "Unassigned"
defaulting. Field nullability therefore mirrors the ORM columns directly —
only the ``nullable=False`` columns are non-optional. The two trailing
fields (``reporter_name``/``assignee_name``) are relation-derived and added
by the handler. Gated byte-for-byte by tests/contract (workitems_detail).
WorkItemDetailResponse.title: WorkItemDetailResponse

WorkItemListResponse.description:
Response shape for ``GET /api/workitems/`` (one item per row).

Documents the wire format for OpenAPI/codegen only — the handler returns
plain dicts at runtime, so this is never used to re-serialize. Field
optionality mirrors the dict-building code in ``list_work_items``: the
numeric fields use an ``or 0`` default (never None), the string fields
``assignee``/``sprint``/``epic``/``description`` use literal defaults
("Unassigned"/"Backlog"/""), and the *_id / *_key / date fields are
nullable. ``id`` is stringified (matches ``SlimWorkItem.id``).
WorkItemListResponse.title: WorkItemListResponse

info.description:
AI-powered project management platform with Jira-like boards.

    ## Features
    - **Projects**: Create and manage multiple projects
    - **Kanban Board**: Drag-and-drop work item management
    - **AI Generation**: Auto-generate user stories and tasks
    - **Sprint Management**: Organize work into sprints
info.title: Arsenal Ops - AI Project Management

get.description: API root

Get weekly capacity for all developers across all projects.

Saturday-Friday week. See backend/services/capacity_service.py for the rules.
Response embeds a per-ticket breakdown so the UI can drill down without a
second round-trip.

Get all internal employees/developers. External users (created via
Admin → Users → Add User) are intentionally excluded — they live in the
Users tab, not the Employees tab.

schema.title: Response List Employees Api Admin Employees Get

post.description: Create a new employee/developer

delete.description: Delete an employee/developer and their user account

put.description: Update an employee/developer

get.description: Get all active tickets assigned to an employee across all projects, sorted by priority

schema.title: Response Get Employee In Progress Tickets Api Admin Employees  Employee Id  In Progress Tickets Get

get.description: List every category, ordered by name. Includes ``project_count``.

schema.title: Response List Categories Api Admin Project Categories  Get

post.description: Create a category. Names are unique (case-sensitive).

schema.title: Response Create Category Api Admin Project Categories  Post

delete.description:
Delete a category. Projects pointing at it are auto-unassigned via the
``ON DELETE SET NULL`` FK constraint — no application-level cleanup needed.

put.description: Update a category. Name uniqueness checked against the other rows.

schema.title: Response Update Category Api Admin Project Categories  Category Id  Put

get.description: Get all projects with stats for admin

schema.title: Response List All Projects Api Admin Projects Get

get.description:
Per-project counts of in-progress / in-review / done-this-week.

Filters
-------
- ``?category_id=5`` → only projects in category 5
- ``?uncategorized=true`` → only projects with no category
- both omitted → every project

Query strategy: one query for the project rows themselves, then two
aggregate GROUP BY queries (active statuses + done-this-week). Three
round trips total regardless of project count.

put.description:
Assign / change / clear a project's category from the admin Projects tab.

Gated separately on `admin.projects_write` rather than the general
`update_project` endpoint (which uses `require_project_admin`) so that
read-only admins and per-project admins can't reorganize the admin-wide
categorization. Body: ``{"category_id": <int> | null}``.
Update project GitHub settings

get.description:
Tickets bucketed by status for the Reports → expanded-row drill-down.

All three buckets returned in a single payload so flipping between the
In progress / In review / Done buttons in the UI is a pure client switch
— no extra round trip per click.

Bucket definitions match the report endpoint:
  - in_progress / in_review: snapshot of items in that status right now
  - done_this_week:          status='done' AND completed_at in current week
Get list of available specializations

Get admin dashboard statistics.

NOTE: This used to backfill missing Developer rows for active Users on GET.
That side-effect has been moved to ``backend/scripts/backfill_developers.py``
which must be run once at deploy.

List time entries for the admin Time Entries tab.

Filters (all optional, combined with AND):
  - project_id:  restrict to one project (joined via WorkItem.project_id)
  - developer_id: restrict to one employee
  - date_from / date_to: inclusive ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD). The
    upper bound is treated as end-of-day, so `date_to=2026-06-08` keeps
    entries logged at 23:59:59 that day.

Returns at most TIME_ENTRIES_MAX_ROWS rows ordered by logged_at DESC.

post.description:
Admin: Pre-register a user for Google SSO login.

Everyone authenticates via Google SSO. Internal-domain emails are
auto-provisioned on first SSO login and don't need this endpoint —
it's primarily for authorizing external users (whose domains the SSO
endpoint would otherwise reject) by creating the User row in advance.
No password is issued; hashed_password is left null.

schema.title: Response Create User Api Auth Admin Create User Post

post.description: Admin: Reset a user's password

schema.title: Response 200 List Roles Api Auth Admin Roles Get

get.description: Admin: List all users (with github_username joined from linked Developer).

schema.title: Response 200 List Users Api Auth Admin Users Get

put.description:
Admin: Update a user's profile (name, email, GitHub username).

Email changes are accepted but risky — the user's Google account must match
the new email for SSO to keep working. github_username writes through to the
linked Developer row if one exists; otherwise it's silently ignored.
Admin: Update a user's role

get.description:
Return the static capability registry. Used by the admin role-edit UI.

The registry is process-static (defined in capabilities.py) so we mark the
response as cacheable in the user's browser for 5 minutes to avoid
repeated round-trips when the admin UI re-renders.

post.description: Change password (required on first login)

Issue a JWT for a local admin user without going through Google SSO.

Only enabled when DEV_AUTH_BYPASS=1 is set on the backend process. Idempotent:
on first call, creates a `dev@local` admin (and the matching Developer record
so the user shows up on boards); on subsequent calls, reuses it.

get.description: Lets the frontend decide whether to render the dev-login button.

post.description:
Google SSO Login Endpoint

OAuth 2.0 Flow:
1. Frontend gets ID token from Google Sign-In
2. Frontend sends token to this endpoint
3. Backend verifies token with Google
4. Backend creates/updates user in database
5. Backend returns JWT access token
6. Frontend stores JWT and uses for subsequent requests

get.description:
Get Google Client ID for frontend configuration
Frontend needs this to initialize Google Sign-In

post.description: Login with email and password

get.description: Get current user info

Effective capability set for the calling user (union over their roles).

Cached in-process for 60 seconds, keyed on user id + role id set, so the
frontend can poll this from the layout without pounding the DB. The cache
is cleared whenever a role mutation endpoint runs.

post.description: Create a new comment with @mentions (requires auth)

get.description: Get all business review comments for a project with work item details

schema.title: Response Get Business Review Comments Api Comments Project  Project Id  Business Review Get

Weekly capacity for the currently-logged-in developer (cross-project,
Saturday → Friday UTC). Same shape as a single row from

record yet (e.g., admin-only user).
Get a developer by ID (requires auth)

post.description: Convert a personal task to a project ticket (requires `project.assign_personal_task`).

Upload and analyze a PRD file (PDF or Word) (requires `project.ai.write`).

Analyze PRD from text input (requires `project.ai.write`).
Returns: PRD analysis and generated architectures.

get.description: Get a specific architecture (requires auth)

put.description:
Update an architecture (e.g., edit mermaid code).

Architecture lives inside the Overview section, so writes share the
same gate as other Overview edits — see `is_project_admin` for the
three accept paths (tool admin, `project.overview_write`, per-project
admin). Project is resolved from the architecture row.

post.description:
Use AI to refine an architecture based on user's plain English instructions.
Takes the current mermaid code and change description, returns updated architecture.

Example instructions:
- "Add a Redis cache layer between the API and database"
- "Replace the monolithic backend with microservices"
- "Add authentication service with OAuth2 support"
Select an architecture for the project (requires auth)

get.description: Get the latest PRD analysis for a project (requires auth)
Get all architectures for a project (requires auth)

schema.title: Response 200 Get Project Architectures Api Prd Projects  Project Id  Architectures Get

post.description:
Commit selected architecture and generate Jira tickets.
Assigns tickets to developers based on their specialization.
Divides project into sprints if timeline is provided.

Same Overview-write gate as the other architecture endpoints — also
happens to be the only path that creates work items from the Overview
flow, so requiring project-admin (or `project.overview_write`) here is
a tighter version of the same intent.

Generate a roadmap .xlsx template (requires `project.ai.write`).

If the project has a PRD analysis, the LLM seeds the file with suggested
milestones / epics / tasks. Otherwise the user gets a blank scaffold with
the correct columns and one placeholder milestone — they fill it in and
re-upload via POST /api/roadmap/parse-file.
Preview tickets that would be generated without committing (requires auth)

get.description: Return metadata for the saved roadmap template, or 404 if none exists.
Re-render the saved roadmap template and stream the .xlsx.

List projects (admin sees all, developers see assigned only).

Optional filters:
    - ``?category_id=5`` → only projects in category 5
    - ``?uncategorized=true`` → only projects with no category assigned

The two flags are mutually exclusive; if both are supplied,
``uncategorized`` wins (explicit "no category" beats a numeric id).

schema.title: Response 200 List Projects Api Projects  Get

post.description: Create a new project (requires `project.create`).

get.description:
List project categories (id + name + description only) for users
creating projects. Gated on `project.create` because the only caller is
the Create Project dialog — admins fetch the richer category list
(with `project_count`) via `GET /api/admin/project-categories`.

Returned in alphabetical order so the picker stays predictable.

put.description: Update a project goal
Update a project milestone

post.description: Mark a milestone as completed

delete.description:
Delete a project and its work items.

Restricted to tool-level admins via the `admin.projects` capability —
project-level admins (developers with `is_admin=True` on the project's
membership row) cannot delete the project itself. Deleting a project is
a tool-administration action, not a per-project workflow action. The
capability is held by the `admin` system role (`*`) and any custom role
that explicitly grants `admin.projects`.

get.description: Get a project with work item stats (requires access)

put.description:
Update a project's information.

Restricted to project admins (`is_admin` on this project's membership)
and system admins (`admin.projects` capability). Was previously
`require_project_access` — i.e. any assigned developer — which let regular
team members rename or restatus the project. Now matches the same gate
used by add/remove/promote/demote-developer endpoints.

get.description: Get activity feed for a project

schema.title: Response 200 Get Project Activity Api Projects  Project Id  Activity Get

post.description:
Add a developer to a project.

Restricted to project admins (`is_admin` on this project's membership)
and system admins (`admin.projects` capability). `require_project_admin`
handles the 404-if-no-project and 403-if-not-admin cases and returns the
loaded Project row, so we don't repeat the lookup.

delete.description:
Remove a developer from a project.

Restricted to project admins and system admins — same gate as add/promote.

put.description: Promote a developer to project admin (requires project admin access)
Demote a developer from project admin (requires project admin access)

get.description: Get all files for a project

post.description:
Upload a file to a project.

Resource management (files + links) is part of the Overview section,
so it shares the same gate as other Overview writes — see
`is_project_admin` for the three accept paths (tool admin, overview
write cap, or per-project admin).

delete.description: Delete a file from a project. Gated like other Overview writes.

get.description: Download a file from a project

Send GitHub repository invitations to all project developers.
Uses project-specific GitHub token if configured, otherwise uses global GITHUB_TOKEN.
(requires auth)

get.description: Check GitHub integration status for a project (requires auth)
Get all goals for a project

schema.title: Response 200 Get Project Goals Api Projects  Project Id  Goals Get

post.description: Create a new project goal

get.description: Get all links for a project

schema.title: Response Get Project Links Api Projects  Project Id  Links Get

delete.description: Delete a link from a project. Gated like other Overview writes.

get.description: Get all milestones for a project

schema.title: Response 200 Get Project Milestones Api Projects  Project Id  Milestones Get

post.description: Create a new project milestone

get.description:
Bundle of the data the project landing page needs in a single request.

Replaces the prior 7 parallel calls with one. Each sub-section falls back
to a sensible empty value on error so the page still renders.

Return DB-derived values aligned 1:1 with the frontend ``PulseData``
shape. Each sub-section falls back to a sensible empty value on error
so the page still renders even if one computation breaks. The
``_meta.degraded_sections`` list surfaces which (if any) sections fell
back so the frontend / ops can flag partial responses.

Return the editorial Pulse blob for a project.

If no row exists, returns ``{"data": {}, "updated_at": null,
"updated_by": null}`` — the frontend uses that as the "first-time"
signal to do a one-shot localStorage migration.

put.description:
Upsert the editorial Pulse blob for a project.

The blob is opaque on the server — the frontend types are the
contract and they evolve faster than the backend should. The PUT
handler captures the calling user into ``updated_by_user_id`` so
the UI can show "last edited by X".

Access control mirrors the frontend ``project.pulse.settings``
capability: only project admins (or system admins) can edit. Any
project member can still read via the GET handler above.

Note: last-write-wins; multi-PM editing is rare for editorial blobs
so we deliberately don't add optimistic locking here.

get.description:
Get workload + Sat-Fri weekly capacity for all assignees in this project.

Capacity rules (status-based, transfer-aware) live in
backend/services/capacity_service.py — same logic as the admin capacity endpoint.

post.description:
Create work items from parsed roadmap data (requires `project.ai.write`).
Creates epics first, then tasks linked to epics.
Handles assignee lookup with fallback to current user.

Upload and parse a roadmap Excel file (requires `project.ai.write`).
Returns: Summary of epics, tasks, assignees, timeline, conflicts, warnings, and sprints.

get.description:
All of the current developer's positioned blocks that START within the
7-day window beginning at ``week_start`` (UTC). Legacy entries without a
``start_time`` are excluded — the UI surfaces those in its unscheduled tray
via the per-ticket time-entries endpoint.

post.description: Create a positioned block on a ticket the caller is assigned to.

delete.description: Delete a block and re-roll the ticket's hours.

patch.description:
Move, resize, or reassign a block. Reassignment rolls up hours on both
the previous and the new ticket.

get.description:
List all work items with optional filters (requires auth).

Supports pagination via ``limit`` (1..1000, default 500) and ``offset``
(default 0). Total row count is returned in the ``X-Total-Count`` response
header so the frontend can render a paginator without a second request.

schema.title: Response 200 List Work Items Api Workitems  Get

post.description: Create a new work item (requires `project.tracker_write`).

put.description: Batch update status for multiple work items (requires auth)

get.description:
Slim variant of ``GET /api/workitems/`` used by the Kanban board.

Skips the description/acceptance_criteria/date columns the board never
renders. The parent/epic key batch lookup mirrors ``list_work_items`` so
callers still see human-readable identifiers without per-row joins.

parameters.description: Optional: filter to a single sprint
parameters.name: sprint_id

schema.title: Sprint Id
Response List Board Items Api Workitems Board Get

Generate work items using LLM agent (requires auth).

Returns *unsaved* draft items for the client to review; nothing is

(``{key_prefix}-{n}``) generated by an in-function counter.

get.description: Get all tasks assigned to the current user across all projects

schema.title: Response 200 Get My Tasks Api Workitems My Tasks Get

get.description: Get project analytics for charts and graphs (requires auth)

Get hours analytics for project manager view.
Authorized for users with the project.pm capability OR project-level admin
membership on this specific project (matches frontend canSeePMTab logic).

Debug endpoint to diagnose hours calculation issues.
Shows detailed breakdown of all time entries and their attribution.

post.description:
Repair endpoint to fix hours calculation inconsistencies.
Syncs work_item.logged_hours with sum of time_entries.

Parameters:
- dry_run: If True, only shows what would be changed without making changes

Requires admin access.

get.description: List all sprints for a project with work item counts (requires auth)

schema.title: Response 200 List Project Sprints Api Workitems Projects  Project Id  Sprints Get

post.description: Create a new sprint (requires `project.tracker_write`).

get.description: List sprints, optionally filtered by project (requires auth)

delete.description: Delete a sprint; work items are unassigned (sprint_id → NULL) (requires auth)

get.description: Get a specific sprint with its work items (requires auth)

put.description: Update sprint fields (requires auth)
Activate a sprint (requires auth)
Complete a sprint (requires auth)

get.description:
Get a specific work item (requires auth).

Augments the raw model with `reporter_name` and `assignee_name` so the
side panel can show "Created By" / "Assigned To" without a follow-up
lookup. The model's column dict is returned as-is otherwise.

put.description: Update an existing work item (requires auth)

get.description: Get all dependencies for a work item

post.description: Add a dependency to a work item

delete.description: Remove a dependency from a work item

post.description:
Log hours to a work item - creates time entry and updates totals.

Authorization: only the developer the ticket is assigned to may log hours
on it. The caller is matched to a Developer row by email; if that developer
isn't the assignee, the request is rejected. Tickets with no assignee
cannot be logged against by anyone.

put.description: Move a ticket to a different sprint or to backlog (requires auth)

get.description: Get time entries for a specific work item (requires auth)

post.description:
Resolve every unresolved blocker comment on this ticket in one shot.

"Blocked" in Arsenal Ops isn't a separate work-item field — it's
derived from whether the ticket has any open `comment_type='blocker'`
comments. Unblocking flips them all to `is_resolved=True` so the
blocker chronology is preserved (the comment text + @mentions stay
visible, just marked as resolved). An activity row records the bulk
unblock so the audit trail captures who unblocked + when.

Returns the number of comments resolved — 0 is a legal response when
the ticket was already unblocked (idempotent).
